rulings.Case Title: MOBILEONE LLC VS. FIRST WESTERN PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Scott Laeber, Colliers International Ca, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 7, 2021, line 2. Defendant Scott Laeber, Colliers International Ca, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint Demurrer to causes of action for indemnity is moot; dismissal on file. Demurrer is otherwise sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to the specific duty to investigate zoning. References made in opposition to general statutes establishing obligations of the brokers and general references to the Letter of Intent/Lease are not sufficient. The issue here is not the existence of a client/broker relationship, the issue is the specific duty to investigate zoning - the source of this duty is not currently alleged. Plaintiff shall further allege misrepresentation with particularity. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MOBILEONE LLC VS. FIRST WESTERN PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Angela Oliveri, First Western Properties, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 7, 2021, line 2. Defendant Angela Oliveri, First Western Properties, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint Demurrer to causes of action for indemnity is moot; dismissal on file. Demurrer is otherwise sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts showing the existence of a written contract or establishing exception to the statue of frauds. Plaintiff shall also allege facts in support of each element of breach of fiduciary duty. The Court will not follow Afont at this time, but may do so when the cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is properly asserted if, as such, it appears completely duplicative of the cause of action for professional negligence. Plaintiff shall further allege misrepresentation with particularity (when, how, by what means, where, etc), especially as against a corporate defendant. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MOBILEONE LLC VS. FIRST WESTERN PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
